page: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU, and is bundled with 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate a significant divergence between single-threaded and multi-threaded capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is positioned in the top tier, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show strong results. Conversely, multi-core stress-ng performance is weak, and the overall PassMark CPU score is poor. Large-block memory bandwidth is average, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are also weak to poor.

This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute power. The primary tradeoff is the shared single-core CPU allocation, which limits parallel processing and high-throughput database operations. This server is suited for lightweight memory-cached applications, single-threaded processing tasks, and development environments that do not require dedicated CPU resources.
description: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput are weak to poor due to the single shared vCPU limitation. Large-block memory bandwidth remains average. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio, offering qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads. It is suited for lightweight memory-cached applications, development environments, and basic single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-1c-8gb-150s-amd is a virtual server featuring a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 150 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-threaded performance for memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A single-core virtual server combining a high memory-to-vCPU ratio with local NVMe storage for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
